examples: SAW MILL RIVER RD @ PALMER RD -> Saw Mill River Rd @ Palmer Rd
MAPLE AVE @ MUNICIPAL PL -> Maple Ave @ Municipal Pl
KINGS FERRY RD @ POST HAMOCK HOUSE -> Kings Ferry Rd @ Post Hamock House
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

examples: 40 - MT VERNON-WHITE PLAINS-WC MC -> 40 - Mt Vernon-white Plains-wc Mc
20 - THE BRONX-WHITE PLAINS -> 20 - The Bronx-white Plains
fixes.label: Recased shouting route names

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 4142 of 4142 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 2640 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 100% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
findings.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
findings.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_stop' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for stop (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'route_long_name_contains_short_name' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Route long name contains short name (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'fast_travel_between_far_stops' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Fast travel between far stops (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_headsign_matches_intermediate_stop' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip headsign matches intermediate stop (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 9 kinds of issue across 48473 instances (0 error, 48470 warning, 3 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 75 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 80 days.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 80 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 57 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (40, 4, 20, 7, 2, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 2754 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("SAW MILL RIVER RD @ PALMER RD", "MAPLE AVE @ MUNICIPAL PL", "KINGS FERRY RD @ POST HAMOCK HOUSE", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.
